messages.content: Hey! 😊 I’ve been looking for some new podcasts to listen to. Anyone have any favorites they’d recommend?

Oh nice! I’m always hunting for new podcasts too. Lately I’ve been into “Heavyweight”—it’s funny and kinda heartwarming, all about people revisiting moments from their past. Also, “Science Vs” is great if you like digging into myths and facts about random topics. What kind of stuff are you into?

Ooh, I’ve heard good things about Heavyweight but haven’t tried it yet! Science Vs sounds right up my alley though—I love nerdy deep dives 😂 I’m into a mix, honestly… true crime, pop culture, and I’m a sucker for anything with good storytelling. Lately, I’ve been obsessed with “Criminal” and “Normal Gossip”—they’re both super bingeable. Let me know if you want more recs!

Yes, please hit me with all your recs! I like something I can get lost in during long walks—I’m always looking for an excuse to stay out longer 😂 Criminal is so good; I binged a bunch of episodes last winter. Haven’t tried Normal Gossip yet, but it sounds fun. Anything in the pop culture lane you’d recommend?

Ohhh for pop culture, you might like “You’re Wrong About”—they dig into misunderstood news stories and public figures, and the hosts are hilarious. Also, “Pop Culture Happy Hour” is a solid go-to for TV, movies, and music talk, and the episodes are pretty short so it’s easy to squeeze in. I totally get it about the long walks… podcasts make them 10x better! Let me know if you try any of these!

You’re Wrong About has been on my list forever! Might finally give it a shot—love when hosts have good chemistry. Pop Culture Happy Hour sounds perfect too; sometimes I just want something quick and chill. 

Have you ever heard of “Reply All”? It’s technically about the internet but they go down some wild rabbit holes—it’s never what you expect. If you’re ever in the mood for something weird and fascinating, I’d recommend it! Any hidden gems you’ve stumbled on that aren

Reply All is such a classic! Some of their episodes are legit wild—I still think about the one with the missing song, lol. For hidden gems, have you tried “Decoder Ring”? They do deep dives into really random cultural mysteries (like why we started eating ice in drinks). Also, “Off Menu” is a fun British one where comedians talk about their dream meals—it’s weirdly comforting. Always happy to swap more recs if you want!

Decoder Ring sounds awesome—I love weird cultural rabbit holes like that! And Off Menu is new to me, but it sounds like a fun vibe. I’ll have to check those out (I’m always here for British humor and food talk 😂).

If you ever want something a little more offbeat, I weirdly enjoyed “Ologies”—it’s just the host talking to all kinds of scientists about their specific “-ology.” Super random but surprisingly addictive. Happy to swap more anytime, my

Omg, I LOVE Ologies! Alie Ward is so funny, and it always makes me feel smarter listening to all the random science stuff. I was obsessed with the “Mycology” episode for weeks after, lol. I could honestly talk podcasts all day, so hit me up anytime you find a new one! I’ll let you know if I stumble on any other hidden gems too.

Haha, glad we’re on the same page—Ologies is a treasure. That mycology episode made me want to go out and find mushrooms (even though I’d probably just find some weird weeds 😂). 

I’ll definitely let you know if I find anything binge-worthy. If you ever get in the mood for some audio fiction, “Welcome to Night Vale” is a classic, but “Passenger List” is a cool thriller too. Anyway, thanks for all the recs—my

Yesss, Night Vale is so good! I went through a phase where I listened to it before bed and it made my dreams super weird 😂 I haven’t tried Passenger List yet, but I’m adding it to my queue now. I love a good thriller! If you’re ever in the mood for something a little spooky but not too scary, “Spooked” has some wild real-life ghost stories. Seriously, our podcast lists are gonna be out of control at this rate!

Haha, right? My queue is so long I’ll never catch up, but I’m not mad about it 😅 Spooked sounds awesome—I love creepy stories as long as I’m not listening alone at night. Might need to save that one for daytime walks!

Have you ever tried “Song Exploder”? Musicians break down how they made specific songs, and it’s super cool if you like hearing the behind-the-scenes creative stuff. Also, random question—do you ever re-listen to

Oh, I love Song Exploder! It’s so cool hearing artists talk through their process—sometimes it totally changes how I hear a song. The one with Mitski was awesome. And yes, I totally re-listen to my favorites, especially the really comforting ones or anything that makes me laugh (like early No Such Thing As A Fish episodes). Sometimes I’ll even re-listen to old episodes if I need background noise while I work. Do you have any you go back to?

Definitely! I re-listen to Ologies episodes a lot, especially when I need something both interesting and chill—her voice is just super soothing. I’ll also throw on old Criminal or 99% Invisible episodes if I’m cooking or cleaning, because they’re always solid no matter how many times I hear them. 

Oh, and No Such Thing As A Fish is such a comfort show! I’ll have to dig into the earlier episodes, I haven’t gone that far back yet.

You totally should! The early Fish episodes are a little more chaotic but in a fun way—it’s like hanging out with a bunch of friends who know the weirdest facts. Criminal and 99% Invisible are classics too; I feel like they make me smarter without even trying 😂

Have you ever listened to “Heavyweight”? It’s kind of emotional but also really funny—each episode is about someone trying to resolve something from their past. Super bingeable and weirdly uplifting. Let me know

Heavyweight is awesome! I love how Jonathan Goldstein always manages to make things both awkward and heartwarming at the same time. Some of those episodes get me right in the feels—perfect mix of funny and meaningful. 

By the way, if you ever want something totally random and light, “The Anthropocene Reviewed” is a nice listen—John Green reviews random parts of the human experience (like Diet Dr. Pepper or scratch & sniff stickers), and it’s way more moving than you’d

Ahh yes, The Anthropocene Reviewed is so good! John Green’s voice is just so calming and somehow makes even the most random things feel deep. I still think about that episode where he reviews the plague and velociraptors in the same breath 😂

Honestly, podcasts like that are the best for long walks or when you just need a little perspective shift. If you ever want another one in that vein, “Everything Is Alive” is hilarious and oddly touching—each episode is an interview with
Haha, I’ve heard of Everything Is Alive but never listened—an interview with inanimate objects, right? That concept cracks me up. I can totally imagine zoning out to t

needle_message: Yes, please hit me with all your recs! I like something I can get lost in during long walks—I’m always looking for an excuse to stay out longer 😂 Criminal is so good; I binged a bunch of episodes last winter. Haven’t tried Normal Gossip yet, but it sounds fun. Anything in the pop culture lane you’d recommend?
